title: Keep routine utilities compact
statement: Routine inspection and operational utilities default to compact icon-only controls with concise tooltips and accessible names; reserve labeled buttons for primary workflow transitions.
rationale: This reduces label noise in dense operational surfaces while preserving discoverability and a clear primary-action hierarchy.

prefer: Use a semantically exact icon, accessible name, and concise tooltip.
Use a labeled button for the one action that advances the primary workflow.
avoid: Large labeled controls for refresh, retry, view output, open source, or similar inspection utilities.
Icon-only treatment for unfamiliar, destructive, or primary actions.

when: The action is routine, local, reversible, and repeatedly used in a dense surface.
not_when: The action is unfamiliar, destructive, or the primary transition.
The icon cannot express the action precisely.

exceptions.condition: The action is primary, destructive, unfamiliar, or difficult to represent precisely.
exceptions.use_instead: Use a concise labeled control and the appropriate confirmation or recovery treatment.

evidence.summary: Asked to mine thread history into a personal doctrine, illustrating compact icon-only utility controls with tooltips.
Asked automation-row controls to stay hidden until row hover, while the drag affordance remains discoverable beforehand.
Requested a compact comment popover with inline counts, no carets, and navigation back to the anchored gutter.

confidence.note: Bootstrapped from independent direct user episodes under the owner's request to mine durable design rules.

lifecycle.decision_note: Activated only for the owner-requested initial bootstrap because repeated independent direct instructions or corrections establish this decision boundary.

positive_examples: Use a semantically exact icon, accessible name, and concise tooltip.
Use a labeled button for the one action that advances the primary workflow.
negative_examples: Large labeled controls for refresh, retry, view output, open source, or similar inspection utilities.
Icon-only treatment for unfamiliar, destructive, or primary actions.

checks: Are routine utilities compact without competing with the primary action?
Does every icon-only action have an accessible name and concise tooltip?
Would a first-time user understand any action that remains unlabeled?
